claims.claim: 100% formal proof verification rate on 100 random instances for Bound Composition Theorem
claims.section: Section 4.2 (Z3-Verified Proof), Table 1, Section 7.2 (Formal Proof Verification), Table 4
claims.notes: FormalProofEngine encodes 6 QF_LRA obligations, generates 100 random probability distributions and bound parameters per obligation, asserts negation of each lemma; Z3 returns UNSAT for all 600 checks. Uses exact rational arithmetic (Python Fraction).

claims.claim: 6 QF_LRA proof obligations all verified (Restriction Soundness, Separator Marginalization, Lipschitz Error Propagation, Running Intersection Validity, Gap Monotonicity, Bound Aggregation)
claims.section: Section 4.2 (Z3-Verified Proof), Appendix A (Full Proof), Table 1
claims.notes: 13 test methods: test_single_instance_all_verified, test_gap_bound_correct, test_restriction_soundness_lemma, test_local_containment_lemma, test_lipschitz_error_lemma, test_monotone_fixed_point_lemma, test_global_validity_lemma, test_random_instances_high_verification_rate, test_proof_result_summary, test_edge_case_single_subgraph, test_interval_arithmetic_soundness, test_discretization_composition_coupling, test_parametric_verification.

claims.claim: Discretization O(1/n) convergence with all bounds valid; empirical rate O(n^{-0.99}) across 7 bin counts
claims.notes: Tests bins n in {5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500}. Fits power law TV = C * n^{-alpha}, yields alpha = 0.99. All bin counts produce valid bounds. DiscretizationErrorAnalyzer also provides per-variable and global error bounds. Tests in tests/test_discretization_error.py (9 tests including test_convergence_experiment, test_full_verification, test_corrected_bounds_wider).

claims.claim: SMT discretization verification certifies TV bound TV(P, P_n) <= M/(2n) for all tested configurations
claims.notes: Encodes non-negativity, normalization, density bound, and negation of TV bound as QF_LRA formula. Z3 returns UNSAT. Also verifies per-variable TV (_verify_per_variable_tv), TV subadditivity (_verify_tv_subadditivity), gap contribution (_verify_gap_contribution), and corrected bounds (_verify_corrected_bounds). Tested in tests/test_discretization_error.py::test_full_verification.

claims.claim: DAG sensitivity analysis via MEC enumeration; MEC sizes 4-16 for chain DAGs with 3-5 nodes
claims.notes: DAGSensitivityAnalyzer._enumerate_mec() enumerates all DAGs in the Markov equivalence class. analyze_mec_sensitivity() computes MEC-robust bounds [L^MEC, U^MEC]. adversarial_perturbation() tests edge flips/additions/removals. Tests in tests/test_sensitivity.py (10 tests including test_chain_dag_sensitivity, test_adversarial_perturbation, test_perturbation_types, test_full_evaluation).

claims.claim: Interval arithmetic soundness proof bridging floating-point and exact rational arithmetic
claims.notes: Verifies that IEEE 754 directed-rounding interval arithmetic produces sound enclosures of exact bounds. Cross-validates against Python Fraction exact arithmetic for small instances. Tested in tests/test_formal_proof.py::test_interval_arithmetic_soundness.

claims.claim: Parametric gap bound: bounds remain valid under small perturbations to input parameters
claims.section: Section 7.5 (Verification Certificate Summary), item 3: Parametric robustness
claims.notes: Certifies that computed bounds remain valid under small perturbations to edge weights and default probabilities. Tested in tests/test_formal_proof.py::test_parametric_verification.

claims.claim: DebtRank contagion model propagates distress through financial network; verified bound of 0.0409 on 50-node scale-free network
claims.notes: DebtRankModel implements compute(), propagate_round(), compute_system_loss(). Supports multiple DebtRankVariant types. Pipeline produces verified DebtRank bound of 0.0409 on 50-node Barabasi-Albert network. Tested in tests/test_network.py and tests/test_integration.py.

claims.claim: Causal polytope LP solver computes tight interventional probability bounds on bounded-treewidth subgraphs
claims.notes: CausalPolytopeSolver constructs causal polytope LP with d^k primal variables and O(k*d) constraints per bag. Uses column generation for large bags (causalbound/polytope/column_generation.py). Also solve_multiple_queries() for batch queries. Tested in tests/test_polytope.py (44 tests).

claims.claim: Tree decomposition of financial network DAG into bounded-treewidth subgraphs
claims.notes: TreeDecomposer.decompose() computes tree decomposition using min-fill heuristic (min_fill_ordering_matrix). Also provides compute_treewidth_upper_bound(), compute_treewidth_lower_bound(), nice_tree_decomposition(), decompose_and_validate(). Tested in tests/test_graph.py (86 tests).

558 test functions across 13 files: test_composition (23), test_discretization_error (9), test_formal_proof (13), test_graph (86), test_instruments (37), test_integration (27), test_junction (76), test_mcts (62), test_network (38), test_polytope (44), test_scm (74), test_sensitivity (10), test_smt (59).

claims.claim: Causal UCB MCTS discovers worst-case intervention scenarios within verified bound region
claims.section: Section 7 (MCTS Adversarial Search with Causal UCB), Algorithm 2
claims.notes: MCTSSearch.search() implements MCTS with CausalUCB.select_action() from causalbound/mcts/causal_ucb.py. Uses d-separation pruning (causalbound/mcts/pruning.py::DSeparationPruner). Tested in tests/test_mcts.py (62 tests).

claims.claim: Discretization-composition coupling proof: total error bounded by sum of individual error terms
claims.notes: Certifies that discretization error and composition gap interact correctly. Tested in tests/test_formal_proof.py::test_discretization_composition_coupling.

claims.claim: Bound Composition Theorem (Theorem 2): composed bound [L*, U*] is sound for all distributions consistent with the causal DAG
claims.notes: CompositionTheorem with TheoremConditions, BoundGuarantee, ValidityProof. Composer in causalbound/composition/composer.py::BoundComposer implements the composition operator from Definition 1. Gap estimation in causalbound/composition/gap_estimation.py::GapEstimator. Tested in tests/test_composition.py (23 tests).

claims.claim: Alethe-format proof certificates extracted from Z3 UNSAT verdicts for independent verification
claims.notes: Converts Z3 proof trees to Alethe format. Certificate emission via causalbound/smt/certificates.py::CertificateEmitter. Tested in tests/test_smt.py (test_create_certificate, test_finalize, test_validate_certificate, test_certificate_serialization, test_chain_certificates).

claims.claim: Contagion models satisfy monotonicity, bounded output, and Lipschitz properties
claims.notes: Verifies 5 properties for contagion models. Linear contagion monotonicity verified via Z3 SMT. Non-linear models use sampling-based verification. Fire-sale model in causalbound/contagion/fire_sale.py, margin spiral in causalbound/contagion/margin_spiral.py, funding in causalbound/contagion/funding.py.

claims.claim: SMT verifier provides streaming co-routine certification of each pipeline step
claims.notes: SMTVerifier with begin/end session, verify_bound, verify_normalization, emit_certificate, context manager. Uses QF_LRA encoding (causalbound/smt/qf_lra.py) and incremental protocol (causalbound/smt/incremental.py). Tested in tests/test_smt.py (59 tests).

claims.claim: Column generation for large causal polytope LPs
claims.section: Section 3.2 (Causal Polytope LP Formulation), paragraph on column generation
claims.notes: ColumnGenerationSolver with MasterProblem, pricing subproblem (causalbound/polytope/pricing.py::PricingSubproblem), Dantzig-Wolfe decomposition (DantzigWolfeDecomposer), column stabilization (ColumnStabilizer). Tested in tests/test_polytope.py.

claims.claim: MEC-robust bounds valid regardless of which DAG in the equivalence class generated the data
claims.notes: MarkovEquivalenceClass and CPDAG representation. DAGSensitivityAnalyzer._enumerate_mec() and _sample_mec() enumerate/sample DAGs. For chain DAGs on k nodes, MEC contains 2^{k-1} DAGs. Tested in tests/test_scm.py (74 tests) and tests/test_sensitivity.py.

known_limitations: Composition theorem proof is via Z3 (not mechanized in Lean/Coq proof assistant)
Contagion model verification uses sampling for non-linear models (not full SMT proof)
Discretization error bounds assume bounded density (may not hold for heavy-tailed distributions)
MEC enumeration is exponential in the number of undirected edges; sampling used for large MECs
Alethe proof extraction depends on Z3's internal proof representation which may change between versions
Test count is 558 (not 564 as may be stated elsewhere); count based on grep of 'def test_' across all 13 test files

tightness_critical: MCTS rollout count and causal UCB parameters
